x-enumeration-literals.name: Allocation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how amounts, benefits, or balances are distributed among accounts, components, or parties.

Comment:
Examples
Principal allocation
Interest allocation
Incentive distribution
Partial rollover allocation

x-enumeration-literals.name: Calculation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how financial values are computed or recalculated.

Comment:
Examples
Interest calculation method
Bonus incentive calculation
Penalty fee calculation

x-enumeration-literals.name: Derivation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies how parameters or values are derived from rules, market data, or product definitions.

Comment:
Examples
Interest rate derived from market rate
Incentive derived from customer tier
Rollover term derived from original deposit

x-enumeration-literals.name: Payment Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how payments or financial transfers are executed.

Comment:
Examples
Interest payout
Incentive payment
Loan repayment method

x-enumeration-literals.name: Processing Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies how and when an operation is processed within the banking system.

Comment:
Examples
Automatic processing
Batch processing
Real-time processing

x-enumeration-literals.name: Application Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how rules or financial actions are applied to a banking arrangement or transaction.

Comment:
Examples
Incentive applied to transactions
Interest applied to balance
Fee applied to account activity

x-enumeration-literals.name: Delivery Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies how outcomes, documents, or benefits are delivered to the customer.

Comment:
Examples
Statement delivery
Account credit
Notification of bonus

x-enumeration-literals.name: Eligibility Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines conditions that determine whether a customer or arrangement qualifies for a service or benefit.

Comment:
Examples
Minimum balance requirement
Customer tier eligibility
Product qualification rules

x-enumeration-literals.name: Timing Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies when a service action or financial event occurs.

Comment:
Examples
Monthly interest calculation
End-of-term rollover
Quarterly incentive distribution

x-enumeration-literals.name: Trigger Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines events that initiate or trigger a banking action.

Comment:
Examples
Deposit maturity triggers rollover
Transaction triggers cashback
Balance threshold triggers incentive

x-enumeration-literals.name: Limitation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines limits or caps applied to a banking feature.

Comment:
Examples
Maximum incentive amount
Minimum deposit rollover amount
Interest rate cap

x-enumeration-literals.name: Validation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies rules used to verify whether operations meet required conditions.

Comment:
Examples
Balance validation
Transaction eligibility validation
Rollover condition validation

x-enumeration-literals.name: Authorization Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines approval requirements before executing an operation.

Comment:
Examples
Customer confirmation required
Bank officer approval
System auto-authorization

x-enumeration-literals.name: Notification Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies how customers are informed about banking events or results.

Comment:
Examples
SMS notification
Email notification
App notification

x-enumeration-literals.name: Adjustment Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies how financial values can be modified or corrected.

Comment:
Examples
Interest adjustment
Fee waiver adjustment
Incentive correction

x-enumeration-literals.name: Pricing Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description: Defines he method, structure, or approach used to determine how a product or service is priced, charged, or billed to a customer.
